questions.prompt: Choose the correct pair of articles: "My uncle is ___ MP and also ___ university lecturer."
questions.explanation: Sound decides each one. MP is said "em-pee", starting with the vowel sound /e/, so an MP. University starts with the /j/ "yoo" consonant sound, so a university. The vowel/consonant letters point the opposite way — a classic double trap.

questions.prompt: Read: "Sara has two cats and a dog. The dog is small, but the cats are big." Which sentence is TRUE?

options.text: Sara has more cats than dogs.
The dog is bigger than the cats.
Sara has a cat and two dogs.

questions.explanation: Two cats vs one dog means more cats than dogs, so (a) is true. The plural cats and singular a dog set the numbers; (b) and (d) miscount, and (c) reverses the text, which says the dog is small and the cats are big.
The wrong one is "mouses": the (animal) plural of mouse is mice. The others are correct — sheep is unchanged in the plural, wife → wives (-fe → -ves), and key → keys (vowel + y just adds -s).

options.text: There are two woman and three childs here.
There are two women and three children here.
There are two womans and three childrens here.
There are two womens and three child here.

questions.explanation: Both nouns are irregular: woman → women and child → children, each with no extra -s. (a) leaves woman singular and misuses "childs"; (c) and (d) add -s to forms that are already plural or keep child singular after a number.

questions.prompt: Fill the gaps: "___ lions live in Africa, but ___ lion at our zoo is from India."
questions.explanation: The first clause is a general truth about all lions, so it takes a plural with no article: Lions live in Africa. The second names one specific, known animal, so it takes the: the lion at our zoo. This contrasts the zero article (general) with the (specific).

options.text: He plays a guitar every day.
The sun rises in the east.

questions.explanation: The error is (b): with musical instruments English uses the, so it should be he plays the guitar. The others are right — an honest (silent h → vowel sound), the sun (unique thing), and a general plural with zero article (Buses…, plus bus → buses).
